name: Cầu Ba Son (Thủ Thiêm 2)
description: Cable-stayed bridge 1.4km 6 lanes connects Tôn Đức Thắng (D1) to Thủ Thiêm (D2). Renamed from Cầu Thủ Thiêm 2 on Dec 2022.

name: Tuyến Metro số 1 (Bến Thành - Suối Tiên)
description: 19.7km metro line 14 stations (3 underground 11 elevated). First metro in Vietnam. Opened Dec 22 2024.

name: Ga Bến Thành
description: Underground terminal station at Ben Thanh Market. Interchange hub for future Lines 2 3a 4.

name: Ga Nhà hát Thành phố
description: Underground station near Municipal Theatre on Đồng Khởi/Lê Lợi area.

name: Ga Ba Son
description: Last underground station before elevated section at former Ba Son shipyard near Saigon River.

name: Ga Văn Thánh
description: First elevated station after Saigon River crossing on Xa lộ Hà Nội corridor.

name: Ga Thảo Điền
description: Elevated station in Thảo Điền ward on Xa lộ Hà Nội.

name: Ga An Phú
description: Elevated station near An Phú intersection on Xa lộ Hà Nội.

name: Nút giao An Phú
description: Multi-level interchange 3 levels at Xa lộ Hà Nội and HCMC-Long Thành expressway. ~70% complete. Delayed to Q2/2026.

name: Đường R1 Khu đô thị mới Thủ Thiêm
description: Arc Boulevard in Thu Thiem New Urban Area ~3.5km 6 lanes connecting central area to Mai Chí Thọ.

name: Đường R2 Khu đô thị mới Thủ Thiêm
description: Central Lakeside Road in Thu Thiem ~2.5km 4 lanes parallel to R1.

name: Đại lộ Mai Chí Thọ mở rộng
description: Traffic improvements and service road additions along Mai Chí Thọ 4.5km 8-10 lanes. Connects Thu Thiem Tunnel to expressway.

name: Chỉnh trang đường Tôn Đức Thắng
description: Sidewalk renovation drainage improvements traffic flow optimization ~2km D1 riverfront. Associated with Metro Line 1 restoration.

name: Đường ven sông Sài Gòn (đoạn Thủ Thiêm)
description: Saigon Riverside Road (R3) in Thu Thiem ~2km connecting waterfront to internal road network.

name: Cầu Thủ Thiêm 1 (nâng cấp giao thông)
district: Quận Bình Thạnh / TP Thủ Đức
description: Traffic management upgrades on existing Thủ Thiêm Bridge 1 1.2km 6 lanes. Signal and lane optimization post-Metro construction.

name: Cầu Phú Hữu (Rạch Chiếc Vành đai Đông)
description: 4-lane, 541m bridge on Eastern Ring Road over Rạch Chiếc, three arch spans, investment 871 billion VND; renamed Phú Hữu in 2016.

name: Cầu Bình Lợi mới (đường sắt)
description: New 1.3 km railway bridge replacing 118-year-old iron bridge over Sài Gòn River; clearance raised from 1.5m to 7m; opened 14/9/2019.

name: Cầu Long Kiểng (Nhà Bè)
description: 1 km bridge on Lê Văn Lương over Phước Kiểng river, 4 lanes, 589 billion VND; completed after 22 years of delays.

name: Cầu Nam Lý (Đỗ Xuân Hợp)
description: 450m bridge on Đỗ Xuân Hợp over Rạch Chiếc, 4 lanes, 731 billion VND; opened 2/10/2024.

name: Cầu Cây Khô (Nhà Bè – Bình Chánh)
description: 485m bridge connecting Nguyễn Bình (Nhà Bè) to Phạm Hùng (Bình Chánh), 2 lanes, 500 billion VND; opened 30/8/2024, cuts journey from 10km to 500m.

name: Cầu Rạch Đỉa mới (Quận 7 – Nhà Bè)
description: New 318m bridge on Lê Văn Lương replacing 50-year-old iron bridge, 512 billion VND; opened 28/11/2024.

name: Cầu Phước Long (Nhà Bè)
description: 380m bridge on Phạm Hữu Lầu road, 737 billion VND; opened 30/12/2024.

name: Cầu Tăng Long (Lã Xuân Oai, Thủ Đức)
description: 231m double-branch bridge on Lã Xuân Oai over Rạch Trau Trảu, 741 billion VND; first branch opened 28/2/2025.

name: Cầu Bà Hom (Bình Tân)
description: 425m bridge over Tham Lương – Bến Cát canal, 4 lanes, 374 billion VND; opened January 2025.

name: Cầu Tân Kỳ Tân Quý mới (Bình Tân)
description: 385m bridge (83m main span) over canal on Tân Kỳ Tân Quý, 4 lanes, 492 billion VND; opened 21/1/2025.

name: Cầu Long Đại (Long Bình – Long Phước, Thủ Đức)
description: 350 billion VND bridge over Tắc river connecting Long Bình and Long Phước wards; construction restarted 2022, completed 2024.

name: Cầu Ông Bồn (Bưng Ông Thoàn, Thủ Đức)
description: Double-span bridge on Bưng Ông Thoàn street; first span opened 30/9/2024.

name: Cầu Nhơn Trạch (Vành đai 3)
description: Major cable-stayed bridge on Ring Road 3 crossing Đồng Nai River between HCMC and Nhơn Trạch (Đồng Nai); first Ring Road 3 component completed.

name: Cầu Bình Khánh (Bến Lức – Long Thành cao tốc)
description: Cable-stayed bridge on Bến Lức–Long Thành Expressway with highest clearance in Vietnam; construction halted 2018–2023, completed 19/12/2025.

name: Cầu Mỹ Thủy 3 (Đồng Văn Cống, Thủ Đức)
description: 6-lane bridge at Mỹ Thủy interchange on Đồng Văn Cống/Võ Chí Công, part of Ring Road 2 corridor serving Cát Lái Port.

name: Đường Trần Quốc Hoàn – Cộng Hòa (nối T3 Tân Sơn Nhất)
description: 4km, 6-lane new road connecting Trần Quốc Hoàn to Cộng Hòa serving Terminal T3, with 2 underpasses and 1 overpass, 4,848 billion VND.

name: Đường Phạm Văn Đồng (hoàn chỉnh toàn tuyến)
district: Gò Vấp / Bình Thạnh / Thủ Đức
description: 13.6km, 12-lane boulevard from Nguyễn Thái Sơn (Gò Vấp) to Linh Xuân (Thủ Đức), 340 million USD investment; full completion 2015.

name: Đường R3 Thủ Thiêm (ven sông nội khu)
description: 2-lane riverside road inside Thu Thiem New Urban Area, one of 4 internal roads totaling 11.9km; completed as part of Thu Thiem road package.

name: Đường R4 Thủ Thiêm (elevated delta road)
description: 2-lane viaduct/elevated road inside Thu Thiem New Urban Area, part of 4-road internal package with 8 canal bridges.

name: Đường Lương Định Của mở rộng (Thủ Đức)
description: 2.5km upgrade of Lương Định Của from Trần Não to Nguyễn Thị Định, widened to 30m/4 lanes, 826 billion VND; phase 1 complete 2024, phase 2 complete 4/2025.

name: Đường Dương Quảng Hàm mở rộng (Gò Vấp)
description: 2.5km widening of Dương Quảng Hàm to 32m/6 lanes, 2,300 billion VND; full opening 26/4/2025.

name: Đường Quốc lộ 50 mở rộng (Bình Chánh)
description: Nearly 7km widening of QL50 to 34m/6 lanes in Bình Chánh, with new Ông Thìn bridge, 1,498 billion VND; target completion 12/2025.

name: Đường song hành Quốc lộ 50 (Bình Chánh)
description: 4.3km parallel road alongside QL50 in Bình Chánh linking HCMC to Long An, partially opened 2024.

name: Đường Nguyễn Hữu Cảnh nâng cấp chống ngập
description: 3.2km flood-prevention upgrade of Nguyễn Hữu Cảnh, 473 billion VND; road surface raised, drainage improved; completed mid-2021, ended chronic flooding.

name: Đường Võ Nguyên Giáp (đổi tên Xa lộ Hà Nội đoạn cầu Sài Gòn – ngã tư Thủ Đức)
description: 7.79km section of Hanoi Highway expanded from 21m to 142–153m width (16 lanes); renamed Võ Nguyên Giáp in 2023; 4,900 billion VND total.

name: Đường Phan Thúc Duyện – hầm chui cửa ngõ Tân Sơn Nhất
description: Underpass at Phan Thúc Duyện – Trần Quốc Hoàn intersection; 42m covered section, 200 billion VND; opened 10/8/2024, 3 months ahead of schedule.

name: Đường R1 Thủ Thiêm (Crescent Boulevard nội khu)
description: 6-lane main internal road (Crescent Boulevard) in Thu Thiem New Urban Area, 55m cross-section, part of 11.9km internal road package.

name: Đường Vành đai 3 đoạn qua Thủ Đức (cầu cạn 14.7km)
description: 14.7km elevated highway (cầu cạn) section of Ring Road 3 through Thủ Đức, opened for technical use 19/12/2025.

name: Đường Vành đai 3 đoạn Củ Chi – Hóc Môn – Bình Chánh (32.6km)
district: Củ Chi / Hóc Môn / Bình Chánh
description: 32.6km western section of Ring Road 3 through Củ Chi, Hóc Môn, Bình Chánh; base layer completed 19/12/2025; full expressway opening 30/4/2026.

name: Đường Tên Lửa mở rộng (Bình Tân)
description: Widening of Tên Lửa road in Bình Tân district, part of western gateway infrastructure improvements.

name: Đường Chu Văn An (ngã 5 Bình Hòa – cầu Chu Văn An, Bình Thạnh)
description: Road segment from Bình Hòa intersection to Chu Văn An bridge; part of 2024–2025 key project list.

name: Đường Nguyễn Văn Linh mở rộng đoạn Huỳnh Tấn Phát – Nguyễn Thị Thập (Quận 7)
description: 2km section widened from 6 to 10 lanes by Phú Mỹ Hưng, each side expanded 13.25m; completed ~May 2022.

name: Đường Hiệp Thành 43 nâng cấp (Quận 12)
description: 550m upgrade of Hiệp Thành 43 road in Quận 12, total investment 159 billion VND; inaugurated 30/8/2024.

name: Đường Võ Văn Kiệt mở rộng làn xe (Quận 1 – Bình Chánh)
description: Removal of median divider on Võ Văn Kiệt widening by ~7m to improve flow; tree relocation and lane reorganization; 13 billion VND.

name: Đường Nguyễn Thị Định mở rộng (Thủ Đức)
description: Widening of Nguyễn Thị Định (former LT-25) as eastern extension of Mai Chí Thọ Boulevard toward Cát Lái Port; part of Eastern transport corridor.

name: Đường Đồng Văn Cống mở rộng (Thủ Đức)
description: Widening and upgrade of Đồng Văn Cống connecting Cát Lái Port to Ring Road 2 and Mỹ Thủy interchange; key eastern port access road.

name: Cầu vượt Nguyễn Thái Sơn – Nguyễn Kiệm (Gò Vấp/Phú Nhuận)
description: Steel flyover bridge at Nguyễn Thái Sơn – Nguyễn Kiệm intersection, reducing congestion in Gò Vấp–Phú Nhuận gateway.

name: Đường Phạm Văn Chí mở rộng (Quận 6)
description: Road upgrade on Phạm Văn Chí corridor in District 6 as part of western inner-city improvements.

name: Đường Tân Kỳ Tân Quý mở rộng (Bình Tân – Tân Bình)
description: Widening of Tân Kỳ Tân Quý road connecting QL1A to inner city and Tân Sơn Nhất airport area; part of airport gateway improvements.

name: Đường Hoàng Hoa Thám mở rộng giai đoạn 1 (Tân Bình)
description: Phase 1 widening of Hoàng Hoa Thám road in Tân Bình; opened 23/1/2025 as part of Tân Sơn Nhất airport approach improvements.

name: Nút giao An Sương (hoàn chỉnh 3 tầng)
description: 3-level interchange at QL1–QL22 with 830m double tunnel (N1 opened 2018, N2 opened 15/7/2020), 514 billion VND; first 3-level interchange at HCMC's northwest gateway.

name: Nút giao Mỹ Thủy giai đoạn 1–2 (Thủ Đức)
description: Phases 1–2 of Mỹ Thủy interchange: Kỳ Hà 3, Ring Road 2 flyover, left-turn underpass to Cát Lái, Mỹ Thủy 3 bridge (6 lanes); serves 20,000 container trucks/day.

name: Nút giao Mỹ Thủy giai đoạn 3 (Thủ Đức)
description: Phase 3: Ring Road 2 flyover (right branch) and Kỳ Hà 3 right branch, 1,826 billion VND; commenced 14/3/2025, target 4/2026.

name: Nút giao Nguyễn Văn Linh – Nguyễn Hữu Thọ (Quận 7)
description: Twin underpasses HC1+HC2 (each 456m, 3 lanes) at NVL–NHT intersection, 830 billion VND; HC2 opened 10/2024, HC1 opened 30/12/2024.

name: Nút giao An Phú (Thủ Đức) — hầm chui HC1-01
description: Largest interchange in HCMC at 3,400 billion VND; HC1-01 tunnel (455m, 4 lanes) opened 30/6/2025 linking expressway to Mai Chí Thọ; full completion Q2 2026.

name: Nút giao Tân Vạn (Vành đai 3 – Xa lộ Hà Nội)
description: Ring Road 3 / QL1 / Hanoi Highway interchange at Tân Vạn; additional flyover branches being added; full completion 30/4/2026.

name: Nút giao Mỹ Yên (Vành đai 3 – Bến Lức – Long Thành)
description: Interchange connecting Ring Road 3 with HCMC–Trung Lương expressway and Bến Lức–Long Thành expressway; opened 19/12/2025.

name: Nút giao cao tốc TP.HCM – Long Thành – Dầu Giây (HLD, Thủ Đức)
description: Ring Road 3 interchange with HCMC–Long Thành–Dầu Giây expressway (node HLD); technical opening 27/4/2025 linking Nhơn Trạch bridge to expressway.

name: Nút giao Phạm Văn Đồng – Nguyễn Thái Sơn (Gò Vấp)
description: Large roundabout (42m radius) at Nguyễn Thái Sơn junction on Phạm Văn Đồng Boulevard; 13 intersections built along the boulevard.

name: Nút giao Bình Thái (Vành đai 2, Thủ Đức)
description: Diamond/cloverleaf interchange on Ring Road 2 at Võ Nguyên Giáp; steel flyover + underpasses; approved 3/2025, start 12/2025, completion 2027.

name: Nút giao Bình Chuẩn (Vành đai 3 – Mỹ Phước Tân Vạn)
description: Ring Road 3 interchange at Mỹ Phước–Tân Vạn highway; includes underpasses and flyovers; 1.3km length; completion 9/2026.

name: Nút giao Khu A (đường Nguyễn Văn Linh – Quận 7)
description: Grade-separated interchange on Nguyễn Văn Linh at Zone A Phú Mỹ Hưng; the only completed grade-separated intersection on NVL at the time.

name: Nút giao VNU (Trước Đại học Quốc gia, Thủ Đức)
description: 1.8km tunnel at VNU–HCMC intersection on Hanoi Highway, expanding from 7 to 14 lanes; opened late 2019.

name: Nút giao Cộng Hòa – Trường Chinh – Tân Kỳ Tân Quý (Tân Bình)
description: Terminal intersection of the Trần Quốc Hoàn–Cộng Hòa road at C12–Cộng Hòa–Trường Chinh; includes underpass and overpass components.

name: Nút giao Ngã Tư Bình Phước (Quốc lộ 13 – Bình Thạnh/Thủ Đức)
description: Flyover bridge upgrade at Bình Phước intersection on QL13 to relieve congestion; clearance and capacity upgrade part of QL13 corridor works.

name: Hầm chui An Sương nhánh N1 (Quận 12)
description: First branch of An Sương twin tunnels (N1: Trường Chinh → QL22 direction), 415m long, 2 lanes; opened 14/3/2018; part of 514 billion VND project.

name: Hầm chui Phạm Văn Chí (Quận 6)
description: Underpass at Nguyễn Văn Luông–Phạm Văn Chí intersection in District 6, 100 billion VND; completed 2019.

name: Hầm chui Nguyễn Hữu Cảnh (Bình Thạnh)
description: ~460m, 4-lane underpass on Nguyễn Hữu Cảnh near Ngô Tất Tố junction and Thủ Thiêm bridge approach; 3 large pumps for flood prevention.

name: Hầm chui Mỹ Thủy (Võ Chí Công, Thủ Đức)
description: Left-turn underpass from Ring Road 2 to Cát Lái at Mỹ Thủy intersection; part of 838 billion VND phase 1 package; completed 2019.

name: Hầm chui Nguyễn Văn Linh – Nguyễn Hữu Thọ HC2 (Quận 7)
description: Second branch (HC2) of twin underpass at NVL–NHT junction; 456m long, 3 lanes; opened October 2024 (HC1 followed 30/12/2024).

name: Hầm chui An Phú HC1-01 (Thủ Đức)
description: 455m, 4-lane underpass at An Phú interchange linking expressway approach road to Mai Chí Thọ; 342 billion VND; opened 30/6/2025.

name: Hầm chui An Phú HC1-02 (Thủ Đức)
description: 760m, 4-lane underpass at An Phú interchange (final component); connects expressway to Mai Chí Thọ toward Thu Thiem Tunnel direction; thông xe 2/2026.

name: Hầm chui nút giao Ngã năm Phước Kiến (Nhà Bè)
description: New 1,000 billion VND underpass at Phước Kiến 5-way intersection in Nhà Bè, 4 lanes, 100-year design life; construction started 2023.

name: Ga Tân Cảng (Metro Line 1, L1-05)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Tân Cảng area, Bình Thạnh District; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Rạch Chiếc (Metro Line 1, L1-08)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Rạch Chiếc; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Phước Long (Metro Line 1, L1-09)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Phước Long area; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Bình Thái (Metro Line 1, L1-10)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Bình Thái; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Thủ Đức (Metro Line 1, L1-11)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Thủ Đức; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Khu Công nghệ Cao (Metro Line 1, L1-12)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at HCMC Hi-Tech Park; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Đại học Quốc gia (Metro Line 1, L1-13)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 station at Vietnam National University; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Ga Bến xe Suối Tiên (Metro Line 1, L1-14)
description: Elevated Metro Line 1 terminal station at Suối Tiên bus station and depot; opened 22/12/2024.

name: Cao tốc HCMC – Long Thành – Dầu Giây mở rộng (đoạn An Phú – Long Thành)
description: Widening of existing HCMC–Long Thành–Dầu Giây expressway; proposed expansion from 4 to 8 lanes as traffic exceeded design capacity; planning stage.

name: Đường song hành cao tốc HCMC – Trung Lương (Bình Chánh)
description: Parallel service road along HCMC–Trung Lương expressway in Bình Chánh; access improvement works completed 2020.

name: Cao tốc Bến Lức – Long Thành (đoạn qua Nhà Bè)
description: Section of Bến Lức–Long Thành expressway through Nhà Bè; construction restarted 2022 after ODA funding suspension; Bình Khánh bridge completed 19/12/2025.

name: Hầm Thủ Thiêm (đường hầm sông Sài Gòn) — upgrade/operation
description: 1.49km underwater tunnel under Sài Gòn River (largest in SE Asia at opening), 6 lanes; opened 2011; ongoing traffic management upgrades in 2018–2020 period.

name: Cầu vượt Nguyễn Kiệm – Hoàng Minh Giám (Gò Vấp/Phú Nhuận)
description: Steel flyover at Nguyễn Kiệm – Hoàng Minh Giám intersection reducing congestion at this airport-adjacent junction.

name: Cầu vượt N2 nút giao An Phú (Thủ Đức)
description: Flyover N2 at An Phú interchange directing traffic from expressway to Mai Chí Thọ; opened night of 31/12/2025.

name: Cầu vượt Kỳ Hà 4 (Mỹ Thủy, Thủ Đức)
description: 4-lane flyover bridge at Mỹ Thủy interchange (Kỳ Hà 4), supplementing existing Kỳ Hà 3 to improve Cát Lái Port access.

name: Cầu vượt T3 Tân Sơn Nhất (đường nối Trần Quốc Hoàn)
description: 988m overpass in front of T3 terminal on Trần Quốc Hoàn–Cộng Hòa road; part of 4,848 billion VND airport access project.

name: Đường trên cao Vành đai 3 (cầu cạn đoạn Thủ Đức)
description: 12.8km elevated viaduct section of Ring Road 3 through Thủ Đức (elevated highway design); technical opening 19/12/2025.

name: Cầu Bình Gởi (Vành đai 3 vượt sông Sài Gòn, Củ Chi)
description: Ring Road 3 bridge crossing Sài Gòn River at Củ Chi between HCMC and Bình Dương; part of 76km Ring Road 3 project.

name: Nhà ga T3 Tân Sơn Nhất (hạ tầng kết nối)
description: Terminal 3 of Tân Sơn Nhất International Airport; opened 30/4/2025; capacity 20 million passengers/year; 11,000 billion VND; generates major road network demand change.

name: Cầu Kỳ Hà 3 (nhánh phải, Mỹ Thủy GĐ3)
description: Right-branch Kỳ Hà 3 bridge at Mỹ Thủy interchange phase 3; 4 lanes; part of 1,826 billion VND giai đoạn 3.

name: Đường Nguyễn Văn Linh – hầm chui HC1 (Quận 7)
description: First branch (HC1) of twin underpass at NVL–NHT, 456m, 3 lanes; opened 30/12/2024; part of 830 billion VND dual tunnel project.

name: Đường Phan Văn Đáng mở rộng (Nhà Bè)
description: Upgrade of road along Lê Văn Lương corridor in Nhà Bè; part of multi-year infrastructure investment in southern HCMC linking Quận 7 and Nhà Bè to Long An.
